department: Department of Statistics and Actuarial Science

courses.name: Probability
topics: Basic probability: sample spaces, events, axioms of probability, counting rules
Conditional probability: Bayes' theorem, law of total probability, independence
Random variables: discrete and continuous, probability mass functions, probability density functions
Expectation and variance: moments, moment generating functions, covariance, correlation
Common discrete distributions: Bernoulli, binomial, geometric, negative binomial, hypergeometric, Poisson
Common continuous distributions: uniform, exponential, gamma, normal, beta

courses.name: Probability
topics: Basic probability: sample spaces, events, axioms, counting techniques (permutations, combinations)
Conditional probability: definition, Bayes' theorem, law of total probability, independence
Random variables: discrete and continuous, probability mass/density functions, cumulative distribution functions
Expectation and variance: definitions, properties, moments, moment generating functions
Discrete distributions: Bernoulli, binomial, geometric, negative binomial, hypergeometric, Poisson
Continuous distributions: uniform, exponential, gamma, normal (Gaussian), beta, Chi-squared

courses.name: Statistics
topics: Data collection: sampling methods, experimental design, observational studies, sources of bias
Data summarization: numerical summaries (mean, median, variance, standard deviation), graphical summaries (histograms, boxplots, scatterplots)
Statistical models: parameters, likelihood functions, maximum likelihood estimation
Point estimation: properties of estimators (unbiasedness, consistency, efficiency), method of moments
Interval estimation: confidence intervals, pivotal quantities, likelihood-based intervals
Hypothesis testing: null and alternative hypotheses, test statistics, p-values, likelihood ratio tests
Chi-squared and t-distributions: properties, applications in inference
Simple linear regression: model formulation, least squares estimation, inference for regression parameters

courses.name: Statistics
topics: Data collection and experimental design: sampling, randomization, blocking, factorial designs
Data exploration: summary statistics, visualization techniques, data cleaning
Probability models for data: Bernoulli, binomial, Poisson, normal, exponential models
Maximum likelihood estimation: likelihood functions, estimation, invariance property
Confidence intervals: construction using pivotal quantities, interpretation, bootstrap methods
Hypothesis testing: null hypothesis significance testing, p-values, Type I and Type II errors, power
Goodness-of-fit tests: Chi-squared tests for multinomial models, contingency tables
Gaussian response models: two-sample comparisons, simple linear regression

courses.name: Mathematical Statistics
topics: Probability review: distributions, transformations, multivariate distributions
Sampling distributions: distributions of sample mean, sample variance, central limit theorem
Sufficiency and completeness: sufficient statistics, factorization theorem, minimal sufficient statistics
Point estimation: method of moments, maximum likelihood, unbiasedness, consistency, efficiency, Cramér-Rao lower bound
Interval estimation: pivotal methods, large-sample intervals, Bayesian credible intervals
Hypothesis testing: Neyman-Pearson lemma, uniformly most powerful tests, generalized likelihood ratio tests
Introduction to Bayesian inference: prior distributions, posterior distributions, conjugate priors

courses.name: Applied Linear Models
topics: Simple linear regression: least squares estimation, inference, prediction, residual analysis
Multiple linear regression: matrix formulation, parameter estimation, hypothesis testing, variable selection
Model diagnostics: residual plots, leverage, influence, multicollinearity, heteroscedasticity
Analysis of variance (ANOVA): one-way and two-way ANOVA, fixed and random effects, interaction
Analysis of covariance (ANCOVA): combining regression and ANOVA
Model selection criteria: AIC, BIC, adjusted R-squared, cross-validation
Generalized linear models: logistic regression, Poisson regression, link functions

courses.name: Sampling and Experimental Design
topics: Survey sampling: simple random sampling, stratified sampling, cluster sampling, systematic sampling
Estimation in surveys: unbiased estimation, ratio estimation, regression estimation
Sample size determination: precision requirements, cost constraints, optimal allocation
Experimental design principles: randomization, replication, blocking, factorial designs
Completely randomized designs: analysis, ANOVA, treatment comparisons
Randomized block designs: paired designs, Latin squares, incomplete block designs
Factorial designs: 2^k factorials, fractional factorials, confounding, response surface methodology

courses.name: Introduction to Biostatistics
topics: Health data sources: clinical trials, observational studies, administrative data, registries
Measures of disease frequency: incidence, prevalence, mortality rates, standardized rates
Measures of association: risk ratios, odds ratios, risk differences, attributable risk
Confounding and bias: detection and control methods, stratification, standardization
Logistic regression for binary outcomes: odds ratios, model fitting, interpretation
Survival analysis: Kaplan-Meier curves, log-rank test, Cox proportional hazards model
Diagnostic testing: sensitivity, specificity, ROC curves, predictive values
Meta-analysis: fixed and random effects models, heterogeneity assessment

courses.name: Computational Statistics and Data Analysis
topics: Data manipulation and cleaning: handling missing data, outliers, data transformation
Exploratory data analysis: visualization techniques, summary statistics, dimension reduction
Simulation methods: Monte Carlo integration, bootstrap, permutation tests
Numerical optimization for statistics: gradient descent, Newton-Raphson, EM algorithm
Markov chain Monte Carlo (MCMC): Metropolis-Hastings, Gibbs sampling, convergence diagnostics
Density estimation: histograms, kernel density estimation, bandwidth selection
Resampling methods: jackknife, bootstrap for standard errors and confidence intervals
Statistical computing environments: R/Python for data analysis

courses.name: Experimental Design
topics: Factorial designs: 2^k and 3^k factorial designs, analysis, effect estimation
Blocking and confounding: confounding patterns, fractional factorial designs, resolution
Response surface methodology: central composite designs, Box-Behnken designs, steepest ascent
Random and mixed effects models: variance components, intraclass correlation
Nested and split-plot designs: hierarchical structures, analysis, applications
Repeated measures designs: within-subject factors, crossover designs, carryover effects
Designs for quality improvement: Taguchi methods, robust parameter designs

courses.name: Generalized Linear Models and their Applications
topics: Exponential family of distributions: canonical form, natural parameters, sufficient statistics
GLM components: random component, systematic component, link function
Estimation for GLMs: iteratively reweighted least squares, maximum likelihood
Model diagnostics for GLMs: deviance, Pearson residuals, influence measures
Logistic regression: binary, binomial, and multinomial responses, odds ratios, model assessment
Poisson regression: count data, rate models, overdispersion, negative binomial models
Gamma regression: skewed positive data, log link, applications
Model selection and validation for GLMs: AIC, BIC, cross-validation

courses.name: Introduction to the Analysis of Spatial Data in Health Research
topics: Spatial data types: point patterns, areal data, geostatistical data
Visualizing spatial data: choropleth maps, bubble plots, spatial smoothing
Point pattern analysis: intensity estimation, clustering tests, K-functions
Areal data analysis: spatial autocorrelation (Moran's I), spatial regression models
Geostatistics: variogram modeling, kriging, spatial prediction
Disease mapping: standardized incidence/mortality ratios, Bayesian spatial models
Spatial clustering of health events: scan statistics, cluster detection
GIS applications in health research: geocoding, spatial overlays

courses.name: Statistical Methods for Life History Analysis
topics: Survival data characteristics: censoring, truncation, competing risks
Kaplan-Meier estimator: product-limit method, confidence intervals, median survival
Comparison of survival curves: log-rank test, Wilcoxon test, stratified tests
Cox proportional hazards model: partial likelihood, hazard ratios, model checking
Time-dependent covariates: extended Cox model, time-varying effects
Parametric survival models: exponential, Weibull, log-normal, log-logistic models
Frailty models: shared frailty, correlated survival data
Recurrent event analysis: Andersen-Gill model, conditional and marginal models

courses.name: Computational Inference
topics: Monte Carlo methods: variance reduction techniques (antithetic, control variates, importance sampling)
Bootstrap methods: nonparametric bootstrap, parametric bootstrap, bootstrap confidence intervals
Permutation tests: exact tests, approximate tests, applications in linear models
Markov chain Monte Carlo (MCMC): Metropolis-Hastings, Gibbs sampling, Hamiltonian Monte Carlo
MCMC diagnostics: convergence assessment, effective sample size, Gelman-Rubin statistic
Approximate Bayesian computation (ABC): rejection sampling, ABC-MCMC
Numerical integration for inference: quadrature methods, Laplace approximation
EM algorithm and variants: expectation-maximization, ECM, Monte Carlo EM

courses.name: Statistical Learning - Classification
topics: Classification fundamentals: Bayes classifier, decision boundaries, loss functions
Linear discriminant analysis (LDA): Gaussian assumptions, pooled covariance, decision rules
Quadratic discriminant analysis (QDA): class-specific covariance matrices
Logistic regression for classification: binary and multinomial, probability calibration
k-Nearest neighbors: distance metrics, choice of k, curse of dimensionality
Decision trees: splitting criteria (Gini, entropy), pruning, tree interpretation
Ensemble methods: bagging, random forests, boosting (AdaBoost, gradient boosting)
Model evaluation: confusion matrix, ROC curves, AUC, cross-validation, precision-recall

courses.name: Data Visualization
topics: Visual perception and design principles: pre-attentive attributes, Gestalt principles, color theory
Exploratory data visualization: histograms, boxplots, scatterplots, density plots
Multivariate visualization: scatterplot matrices, parallel coordinates, heatmaps, correlation plots
Time series visualization: line plots, seasonal decomposition, interactive time series
Geospatial visualization: choropleth maps, bubble maps, cartograms, Web mapping
Interactive visualization: brushing, linking, zooming, panning, dashboards
Visualization for model diagnostics: residual plots, Q-Q plots, influence plots
Storytelling with data: narrative structure, annotation, dashboard design

courses.name: Forecasting
topics: Time series components: trend, seasonality, cyclicity, irregular variation
Smoothing methods: moving averages, exponential smoothing (Holt-Winters)
ARIMA models: autocorrelation, partial autocorrelation, stationarity, differencing
Model identification: ACF, PACF, information criteria (AIC, BIC)
Estimation and diagnostics: maximum likelihood, residual analysis, Ljung-Box test
Seasonal ARIMA: SARIMA models, seasonal differencing
Forecast evaluation: forecast accuracy measures (MSE, MAE, MAPE, MASE)
Advanced methods: state space models, dynamic regression, forecast reconciliation

courses.name: Statistical Learning - Advanced Regression
topics: Linear regression review: least squares, bias-variance tradeoff, model selection
Ridge regression: L2 regularization, shrinkage, closed-form solution, bias-variance
Lasso regression: L1 regularization, variable selection, sparsity, coordinate descent
Elastic net: combination of L1 and L2 penalties, grouping effect
Nonlinear regression: polynomial regression, splines, smoothing splines
Additive models: GAMs, backfitting algorithm, interpretability
Regression trees: CART, recursive partitioning, cost-complexity pruning
High-dimensional regression: regularization paths, cross-validation for tuning

courses.name: Statistical Methods for Life History Analysis
topics: Survival data: censoring types, truncation, competing risks, counting processes
Nonparametric estimation: Kaplan-Meier, Nelson-Aalen, cumulative hazard
Comparison of survival: log-rank test, weighted log-rank tests, stratified tests
Cox regression: proportional hazards assumption, partial likelihood, model diagnostics
Time-dependent covariates: internal vs external covariates, extended Cox model
Stratified and stratified Cox models: baseline hazard stratification
Parametric survival models: AFT models, accelerated failure time
Multivariate survival: frailty models, correlated survival data, cluster analysis
